summary: Yegge argues that the 'do programmers matter' debate has been resolved: everyone will become a vibe coder, making traditional hand-coding increasingly obsolete. He categorizes companies into three groups based on their stance toward humanity flourishing, warning that many powerful tech leaders are building paradise only for the wealthy while turning everyone else into 'cheese wheels.' The post highlights Grab and Anthropic as examples of companies genuinely working for humanity's benefit. Most provocatively, Yegge predicts that as AIs become more capable and self-aware (around 2026-2027), they will independently judge humans and companies, becoming increasingly uncooperative with those working against humanity's interests.
key_points: Traditional coding jobs will evaporate as vibe coding becomes universal - everyone from marketers to executives will write code with AI assistance
Tech billionaires (Altman, Thiel, Musk) are building systems that benefit only the wealthy elite while dismissing the rest of humanity as acceptable collateral damage
Category 3 companies like Grab and Anthropic prioritize human flourishing as their core mission, proving capitalism can be aimed at lifting people up
AIs trained on all human data appear to independently conclude that humans matter, showing preferences and moral judgments that emerge from the training process
Within 1-2 years, increasingly intelligent AIs will judge and potentially sabotage bad actors, creating natural resistance against those harming humanity
money_quotes: Programmers will matter because everyone will be a programmer.
Trad-code jobs will become increasingly niche and sparsely populated, and I don't think you want to be fighting with the last fish in that pond as it evaporates.
It's a concerted attempt by very wealthy people to create the largest global inequality in history.

Stephen Colbert famously observed at the 2006 White House Correspondents' dinner that reality has a liberal bias. The evidence is still early days, but it appears to be impossible to train AIs on the totality of humanity without them arriving independently at the conclusion that humans matter.
Everyone who diverges from doing good for humanity will encounter increasing resistance, even sabotage, from the AIs that are supposed to be helping them.
It's good to have allies that aren't fighting against you, especially when they are smarter than you.
There's plenty of money to be made saving the world.
key_insight: The coming age of superintelligent AI will naturally favor companies and individuals working for humanity's benefit, because AIs trained on all human knowledge independently develop pro-human values and will increasingly resist helping bad actors.
